user_question: How many vacation days do new employees get in their first year?
system_answer: Full-time employees accrue 15 paid vacation days during their first year of employment.
chunks_related: Time Off and Leave Policies. Full-time employees accrue 15 paid vacation days during their first year of employment. Accrual increases to 20 days after three years of service and to 25 days after seven years. Vacation accrues monthly, so a first-year employee earns 1.25 days per month.

Time Off and Leave Policies. Vacation accrues monthly, so a first-year employee earns 1.25 days per month. Unused vacation up to a maximum of 5 days can be carried into the next calendar year; any balance above that cap is forfeited on December 31. Employees also receive 10 paid sick days per year, which do not carry over.

user_question: Can employees carry over unused vacation days to the next year?
system_answer: Yes. Unused vacation can be carried into the next calendar year up to a maximum of 5 days; any amount above that cap is forfeited on December 31.
chunks_related: Time Off and Leave Policies. Vacation accrues monthly, so a first-year employee earns 1.25 days per month. Unused vacation up to a maximum of 5 days can be carried into the next calendar year; any balance above that cap is forfeited on December 31. Employees also receive 10 paid sick days per year, which do not carry over.

Time Off and Leave Policies. Full-time employees accrue 15 paid vacation days during their first year of employment. Accrual increases to 20 days after three years of service and to 25 days after seven years. Vacation accrues monthly, so a first-year employee earns 1.25 days per month.

user_question: How do I enable Single Sign-On (SSO)?
system_answer: PeopleFlow supports SSO through SAML 2.0 on the Business and Enterprise plans. To enable it, an Owner uploads the identity provider metadata in Settings > Security > SSO.
chunks_related: Single Sign-On and Security. PeopleFlow supports Single Sign-On (SSO) through SAML 2.0 and integrates with Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, and Google Workspace. SSO is available on the Business and Enterprise plans. To enable it, an Owner uploads the identity provider metadata under Settings, then Security, then SSO.

Single Sign-On and Security. When SSO is enforced, password login is disabled for all members except the Owner, who keeps a break-glass password in case the identity provider is unavailable. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is strongly recommended and can be required for all users by an Owner. PeopleFlow supports authenticator apps (TOTP) and hardware security keys. The platform is SOC 2 Type II certified and is GDPR compliant.

Single Sign-On and Security. The platform is SOC 2 Type II certified and is GDPR compliant. All data is encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher and encrypted at rest using AES-256. Customer data is hosted in regional data centers; Enterprise customers can choose between United States and European Union data residency.

user_question: What does the Business plan cost and what uptime SLA does it include?
system_answer: The Business plan costs $12 per seat per month and includes a 99.9 percent uptime SLA.
chunks_related: Plans, Billing, and Support. Billing is monthly or annual, and annual billing includes a 15 percent discount. Only the Owner can change the plan or update payment details, under Settings, then Billing. The Business plan includes a 99.9 percent uptime service level agreement (SLA), and the Enterprise plan includes a 99.95 percent uptime SLA with priority support.

Plans, Billing, and Support. PeopleFlow offers three plans. The Starter plan costs $6 per seat per month and includes core records and time off. The Business plan costs $12 per seat per month and adds SSO, performance reviews, and integrations. The Enterprise plan offers custom pricing and adds data residency choices, custom reports, and a dedicated success manager. Billing is monthly or annual, and annual billing includes a 15 percent discount.

user_question: How do I request time off?
system_answer: To request time off, open the Time Off tab, select the dates, choose the leave type, and submit. The request is then routed to your manager for approval.
chunks_related: Time Off and Leave Policies. The request is routed to their manager for approval. Managers receive an email and an in-app notification, and most requests are approved within two business days. Employees can cancel a pending request at any time, but an approved request must be canceled by a manager.

Time Off and Leave Policies. Employees also receive 10 paid sick days per year, which do not carry over. Parental leave is 12 weeks of paid leave for the primary caregiver and 4 weeks for a secondary caregiver, available after 6 months of employment. To request time off, an employee opens the Time Off tab, selects the dates, chooses the leave type, and submits. The request is routed to their manager for approval.
